title: Energy Flow Tracker
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Animated energy flow diagram tracing energy through four trophic levels with branching losses to respiration, waste, and transfer

learningObjectives: Trace energy through a food chain, accounting for respiration, waste, and transfer at each trophic level
Calculate the percentage of energy available at each successive trophic level
Explain why food chains are limited to 4-5 trophic levels
prerequisites: Understanding of food chains and trophic levels
Basic knowledge of photosynthesis and cellular respiration

keyQuestions: Where does the energy go at each trophic level?
Why is only about 10% of energy transferred from one level to the next?
What limits the number of trophic levels in an ecosystem?
How does the second law of thermodynamics apply to food chains?
commonMisconceptions: Students often think energy is 'lost' or destroyed rather than converted to heat
Students may believe the 10% rule is exact rather than an approximation that varies by ecosystem
Students may confuse energy flow (one-directional) with nutrient cycling (circular)
assessmentOpportunities: Students create a data table tracking energy values at each trophic level
Students calculate how much producer biomass supports one kilogram of apex predator
Students explain why shorter food chains are more energy-efficient
